Any substantive review must flag this.","R1.3":"Two central claims rest on weak or indirect evidence: DBA readability is inferred from query-cost filtering rather than measured directly (the 4.1/5 rating is from a 'small user study' and its scope is unstated), and the schema-documentation contribution is supported only by 'preliminary evidence' of 68% agreement, yet the text asserts value 'well beyond the original synthesis goal.'"},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking me to check the logical and evidential soundness of this paragraph — flagging faulty inferences and unsupported claims — or to review its prose quality and writing style?"}

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph contains a visible logical non-sequitur: Cost Auditor filtering high-cost plans is conflated with human DBA readability ('therefore, DBChat's queries are also more readable'), a conclusion that does not follow from the stated premise. Any substantive review must flag this.
rationale.R1.3: Two central claims rest on weak or indirect evidence: DBA readability is inferred from query-cost filtering rather than measured directly (the 4.1/5 rating is from a 'small user study' and its scope is unstated), and the schema-documentation contribution is supported only by 'preliminary evidence' of 68% agreement, yet the text asserts value 'well beyond the original synthesis goal.'

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking me to check the logical and evidential soundness of this paragraph — flagging faulty inferences and unsupported claims — or to review its prose quality and writing style?
